reports.subtitle: Resident module: prazosin (non-selective) vs silodosin (uroselective) plus guideline-directed BP control

reports.title: Look-Alike Insulin Pens — A High-Alert Device Error (Tresiba vs Fiasp)
reports.subtitle: IIM-style case study + the D.E.F.E.N.D. SOP for staff educators and patient training

reports.title: TBM in Compensated Cirrhosis (Child-Pugh A): A Treatment Module
reports.subtitle: Drop pyrazinamide, add a fluoroquinolone, give the dexamethasone taper — and watch the rifampicin–steroid interaction and the LFTs

reports.title: Post-Thyroidectomy Levothyroxine Start in a Patient with Stable CAD
reports.subtitle: Why a euthyroid-pre-op patient with revascularized, asymptomatic CAD gets near-full replacement, not 'start low, go slow' — dose, TSH target, and the 6-week check

reports.title: Severe Hypothyroidism Before Urgent Oral-Cancer Surgery
reports.subtitle: TSH >100, IV LT4 unavailable — oral loading vs 150 mcg, when fT4 (not TSH) clears the OT, reduce to ~100 mcg now, and the adrenal screen you must not skip

reports.title: Progressive Central Precocious Puberty with Coincidental Primary Hypothyroidism
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: MRI indications, GnRHa timing, and the Van Wyk-Grumbach trap

reports.title: Complex Diabetes Management in Advanced (G5 Non-Dialysis) CKD
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: GLP-1 RA vs SGLT2i vs linagliptin at eGFR 14 — why not to combine incretin therapies, hypoglycaemia-first targets, safe insulin de-escalation, and the wider CKD-G5 agenda

reports.title: Aggressive Non-Functioning Pituitary Neuroendocrine Tumor (PitNET): A Growing Knosp-4 Remnant
reports.subtitle: An Endocrine Fellowship Module: reading the pituitary MRI (Knosp grading, post-op artifact vs remnant), choosing re-surgery vs SRS/FSRT vs medical therapy, cabergoline/octreotide/temozolomide roles, and the replacement-monitoring traps (FT4 not TSH, clinical not cortisol, the wrong testosterone regimen)

reports.title: Primary Hyperparathyroidism in a Young Adult: Adenoma vs Hyperplasia, and the Genetics Question
reports.subtitle: A Resident/Fellowship Module: confirming PHPT and excluding FHH, single-gland adenoma vs four-gland hyperplasia, intra-operative PTH, when (and how) to test genes at age ≤40, and post-parathyroidectomy follow-up (hungry bone vs hypoparathyroidism)

reports.title: Gynecomastia in an Adolescent Male: A Hypogonadotropic Pattern and a Suboptimal Testosterone Script
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: reading the low-T/low-LH pattern, why MRI sella is mandatory despite a normal prolactin, CDGP vs permanent hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, and critiquing 'Testoviron 100 mg monthly' for puberty induction

reports.title: Panhypopituitarism from Hereditary Hemochromatosis: When HFE-Heterozygous Doesn't Add Up
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: iron deposition and the gonadotroph-first pattern, why ferritin >2000 with only HFE-heterozygous prompts exome sequencing, replacing cortisol before thyroxine, FT4-not-TSH titration, phlebotomy, and screening organs and relatives

reports.title: Klinefelter Syndrome (47,XXY) in Adults: Optimising Testosterone and the Lifelong Surveillance Strategy
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: trough symptoms on testosterone undecanoate and enanthate/cypionate, the 12-week vs 10-week vs 21-day debate, correcting B12 and vitamin D, when to shorten the interval (check trough T and haematocrit first), the high-risk 'reset' after loss to follow-up, and the multisystem surveillance KS demands (bone, breast cancer, metabolic, fertility, psychosocial, autoimmune)

reports.title: Pediatric Short Stature and the Diagnostic Utility of Body Proportions
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: U:L segment ratio, arm span:height, proportionate vs disproportionate branching, differential diagnosis, and stepwise workup

reports.title: Severe Hypothyroidism with an Incidental Positive ANA: When NOT to Chase Autoimmunity
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: TSH >100 as the real problem, the low-titre ANA trap in Hashimoto, levothyroxine non-adherence vs malabsorption, who actually needs cortisol testing, B12/vitamin-D correction, and the right referral (endocrinology, not immunology)

reports.title: Iatrogenic Hypothyroidism in Graves Disease: Don't Chase the Labs Into Thyrotoxicosis
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: over-blockade on methimazole (TSH 85, FT4 twice below normal), why a fixed-dose restart and a wait-for-thyrotoxicosis restart are both wrong, titration vs block-and-replace, why FT4/T3 (not TSH) guide dosing, and the short-interval recheck

reports.title: Type 3c (Pancreatogenic) Diabetes with ABPA and Chronic Pancreatitis: Four Problems, One Patient
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: basal-bolus in pancreatogenic diabetes (why sulfonylureas/SGLT2i are hazardous and steroids spike glucose), reading total IgE >2500 in ABPA-S, the steroid+azole regimen and what remission really means, fecal elastase and PERT, and the truth about cancer surveillance in chronic pancreatitis

reports.title: Benign Thyroid Nodule: Debunking Goitrogen Myths and LT4 Overtreatment
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: euthyroid TIRADS 2 / Bethesda II — why iodised-salt and cruciferous avoidance are unsupported, what to counsel instead, and when ATA guidelines forbid LT4 suppression

reports.title: Unexplained Weight Loss in Older Adults
reports.subtitle: Resident Training Module: Critiquing a shotgun workup and applying a stepwise, prevalence-driven diagnostic framework

reports.title: Antibody-Negative Diabetes in an 18-Year-Old: Is It MODY, and Can the Insulin Stop?
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: why young + lean + antibody-negative is not automatically type 1, the C-peptide honeymoon trap, UCPCR and the MODY probability calculator, GCK vs HNF1A and the sulfonylurea trial, and how to come off insulin safely

reports.title: Graves Disease and Urgent Cancer Surgery: Is an Undetectable TSH a Reason to Delay?
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: why normal free T3/T4 (not TSH) defines fitness for GA, why a suppressed TSH alone must not postpone urgent oncologic surgery, the role and limits of raising methimazole and adding a beta-blocker, why Lugol iodine is for thyroidectomy not this commando resection, perioperative ATD continuation and a storm plan

reports.title: Panhypopituitarism Meets Prostate Cancer: Profound Weakness After Stopping Testosterone
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: why normal total T4/cortisol can mislead (free vs total, binding globulins and sex steroids), why central hypothyroidism is titrated to free T4 not TSH, judging glucocorticoid adequacy in ACTH deficiency, the cord-compression red flag, and why testosterone cannot be replaced in active prostate cancer

reports.title: Statin Initiation in a Diabetic Woman with Very High LDL and Hypertension
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: primary-prevention risk stratification, why not to start rosuvastatin 40 or atorvastatin 80 in a statin-naive patient, moderate-intensity first steps, uptitration to LDL <70, and managing TG 267

reports.title: Rosuvastatin vs Atorvastatin for Diabetic Macular Edema — Asking the Wrong Question
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: why no statin molecule is proven superior for DME, why the real adjunct is fenofibrate (ACCORD Eye / FIELD) yet its retinopathy benefit is largely lipid-independent, the renal/drug-interaction caveats, and why lipids are adjunctive to anti-VEGF/laser and glycaemic-BP control

reports.title: Primary-Prevention Lipids in an Indian Diabetic: Drop the Aspirin, Stop Chasing the 6%
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: why aspirin comes off in primary prevention (ASCEND), why doubling a statin only buys ~6% (so add ezetimibe), risk-stratifying the South-Asian diabetic to an LDL target, the oral-to-injectable ladder (ezetimibe/bempedoic acid/PCSK9i/inclisiran), and the four classic OPD lipid errors

reports.title: Escitalopram for 'Any Anxiety': Auditing a Resident's Prescribing Heuristics
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: why anxiety is a differential not a prescription, the over-Rx and under-Rx harms of a fixed 5->10 mg / 3-4-month recipe, the correct escitalopram ceiling (20 mg, not 30), why bedtime-for-everyone and never-combine are both wrong, and an assess->classify->treat->continue->taper framework

reports.title: Complex Hypercalcemia Workup: PTH-Dependent Disease in an 82-Year-Old Man
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: why PTH divides all hypercalcemia, the Resident 1 myeloma error, FHH exclusion before surgery, disproportionately elevated 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D and sarcoid cofactor, localization vs diagnosis, pathological fracture and carcinoma red flags, and the complete pre-operative checklist

reports.title: Resistant Ovary Syndrome (Savage Syndrome): When Follicles Exist but Won't Respond
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: high FSH/LH with high AMH and low estradiol, confirmatory gonadotropin non-response, excluding POI/PCOS/hypothalamic disease and FSH-secreting adenoma, why laparoscopic ovarian drilling fails, and FSHR sequencing vs whole-exome testing

reports.title: Pubertal Gynecomastia That Is Not Physiological: Three Published Cases
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: PAIS masking as persistent gynecomastia (PMC6311465), ectopic aromatase in fibrolamellar HCC (JCEM/PMC4918295), and β-hCG-secreting mediastinal choriocarcinoma (PMC6612059) — hormone patterns, clinical pearls, and mandatory workup triggers

reports.title: Male Hypogonadism: 30 Literature-Based Case Summaries
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: primary vs secondary vs mixed classification, congenital and acquired causes (Kallmann, empty sella, opioids, hemochromatosis, 46,XX DSD, PAIS, CAH, mixed KS + pituitary adenoma), fertility pathways (hCG/FSH/pulsatile GnRH), and 18 board-style MCQs — educational revision from published case reports (R1–R24)

reports.title: Gynecomastia in the Aging Male (≥ 40 Years)
reports.subtitle: Obesity-associated hypogonadism, spironolactone unmasking, and SHBG shifts in cirrhosis

reports.title: Estrogen Excess: A Case-Based Atlas (20 Published Cases)
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module across the seven etiologic buckets — exogenous estrogens/ER agonists, aromatizable androgens & digitalis, peripheral aromatase (obesity/aging/refeeding), familial aromatase excess (AEXS), estrogen-secreting tumours (ACC/Leydig/Sertoli/granulosa), hCG-secreting tumours, and therapeutic hCG — with hormone patterns, the master diagnostic algorithm, and when gynecomastia becomes irreversible.

reports.title: Gynecomastia from Androgen Deficiency or Resistance: A Case-Based Atlas (10 Published Cases)
reports.subtitle: A resident training module across four etiologic buckets — primary/secondary hypogonadism, hyperprolactinemia, congenital & acquired androgen resistance, and drugs that block androgen action; with the gonadotropin-led work-up algorithm and a board-style question bank.

reports.title: Gynecomastia from Systemic, Endocrine & Nutritional Disorders
reports.subtitle: A Case-Based Training Module

reports.title: Drug-Induced, Environmental & Pubertal Gynecomastia: A Case-Based Atlas
reports.subtitle: Calcium-channel blockers, SSRIs, PPIs & chelators; endocrine-disrupting chemicals; and pubertal gynecomastia management

reports.title: Low BMI in a Young Adult Woman: A Multisystem Workup Refinement
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: recurrent chest infections + BMI 15–16 + amenorrhea + micronutrient deficiencies with normal stool frequency — immunodeficiency and atypical CF, TB/IGRA, celiac with total IgA, occult malabsorption, FHA vs organic causes, eating-disorder screening, and the sequenced Indian-context algorithm

reports.title: Visa Medical Clearance: Incidental Thyroid Ultrasound and the Case Against Over-Investigation
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: mild heterogeneous echotexture (?thyroiditis) in an asymptomatic euthyroid 24-year-old — why Anti-TPO, ESR, and CRP will not change fitness, how to counter defensive bureaucracy, and drafting a definitive endocrinology clearance letter

reports.title: Irregular Cycles, Subclinical Hypothyroidism, and a 4.5 cm Adnexal Cyst in VWD Type 2
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module (RTM-21/female): TSH 10 with PRL 54 — treat LT4 first and defer pooled prolactin; simple cyst expectant management; LH:FSH >2 and 13 kg weight gain; Meprate bleeding risk and cyst rupture in VWD type 2; staged steroid panel only after thyroid and prolactin normalize

reports.title: Incidental Thyroid Ultrasound in Euthyroid Patients: Diagnostic Restraint (5-Case RTM)
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: five comparative vignettes — visa euthyroid heterogeneity (no testing), SCH with TPO, de Quervain thyroiditis, incidental nodule FNA, and preconception TPO surveillance; master algorithm; when TPO/ESR/CRP are and are not indicated

reports.title: Leydig Cell Hypoplasia (LCH): Two Published Cases of LHCGR Mutations in 46,XY DSD
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: early childhood LCH Type 1 (Çömlek 2021) and complete pseudohermaphroditism with V144F ER-retained LHR (Richter-Unruh 2004) — hCG stimulation failure, inguinal gonad discovery, and LHCGR sequencing in the 46,XY DSD workup
reports.category: Pediatric Endocrinology / Disorders of Sex Development

reports.title: Clinical Evaluation of Gonadal Dysgenesis: Five-Case Resident Training Module
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: genetic → gonadal → phenotypic sex; Swyer syndrome and MGD across five published cases — hCG stimulation, CAH misdiagnosis trap, FISH for idic(Y) mosaicism, laparoscopic histopathology, and gonadoblastoma risk (~20–30%)
reports.category: Pediatric Endocrinology / Disorders of Sex Development

reports.title: 17-Hydroxyprogesterone in the Newborn: Pitfalls in Ruling Out or Diagnosing CAH
reports.subtitle: A Fellow Training Module: five-coordinate 17-OHP interpretation, false-positive/false-negative patterns, 17 published-case teaching set, diagnostic algorithm, and board-style MCQs — do not use 17-OHP as a stand-alone rule-out

reports.title: Biochemically Resistant Microprolactinoma: Escalation, Echocardiography, and Surgery
reports.subtitle: An Endocrine Fellowship Module: DA-agonist resistance after PEG precipitation; pseudo-resistance exclusion; cabergoline titration with mandatory echo monitoring; TSS for a 3 mm microadenoma; symptom management when biochemistry cannot be normalised

reports.title: Klinefelter Syndrome (47,XXY): Lifelong Follow-Up and TRT Monitoring Protocol
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: hypergonadotropic hypogonadism and post-pubertal gynecomastia on Inj Cernos 1000 mg; CBC/haematocrit and LFT surveillance; guidelines-based lifelong monitoring calendar from diagnosis to adulthood

reports.title: Secondary Osteoporosis in a 33-Year-Old Woman — Vimla's Case
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: POI, thyroid dysfunction, and normal cortisol — secondary osteoporosis exclusion algorithm and treatment sequencing (HRT, thyroid optimisation, bone-specific therapy)

reports.title: The Hypokalemia Paradox — CETAE Resident Training Module
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: refractory hypokalemia on 300 mEq/day K+, aldosterone 1850 with unsuppressed renin, falsely low spot urine K+/TTKG — dilutional deception, nocturnal kaliuresis from BPH obstructive uropathy, and split-collection proof

reports.title: Pancreatic NET Tail Resection — PTH-Independent Hypercalcemia Cured Post-Op
reports.subtitle: A Resident Training Module: ectopic 1α-hydroxylase from pNET (1,25-D 154), FDG + Ga-68 DOTANOC staging, immediate normocalcemia after resection, CgA surveillance baseline, prior AKI, and hypertensive urgency at follow-up
